steps.title: Isolate the radical and square both sides
steps.content:
Subtract 4 from both sides to isolate the radical:
$$\sqrt{2x + 6} = x - 1$$ Square both sides to eliminate the radical:
$$2x + 6 = x^2 - 2x + 1$$

steps.title: Solve the resulting quadratic
steps.content:
Rearrange to standard form:
$$x^2 - 4x - 5 = 0$$ Factor:
$$(x - 5)(x + 1) = 0$$ Candidates: $x = 5$ or $x = -1$.

steps.title: Check candidates in the original equation
steps.content:
For $x = 5$:
$$\sqrt{2(5) + 6} + 4 = \sqrt{16} + 4 = 4 + 4 = 8$$ $$5 + 3 = 8$$ ✓

For $x = -1$:
$$\sqrt{2(-1) + 6} + 4 = \sqrt{4} + 4 = 2 + 4 = 6$$ $$-1 + 3 = 2$$ ✗

Only $x = 5$ satisfies the original equation, so the solution set is $\{5\}$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
